_meta.description: UI feature list for the AgentTown escape room UI rework. Features are ordered by priority (P0 first). The 'passes' field is set ONLY by the ui-reviewer agent.

features.name: React + Vite scaffold
features.description: Initialize a React + TypeScript + Vite project in frontend/. Install Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Zustand, and React Router. Configure Vite to proxy API requests to localhost:8741. Add a minimal App.tsx with router shell and a health-check fetch to /api/state.
features.notes: Reviewed 2026-03-26. All criteria met. Build clean, theme correct, health check robust.

features.name: FastAPI serves built frontend
features.description: Update agenttown/server.py so that GET / serves the Vite-built index.html from frontend/dist/ (with static asset serving). Keep the existing /dashboard endpoint as a fallback to the old embedded UI. Add a dev note in README or CLAUDE.md about running both dev servers.
features.notes: Reviewed 2026-03-26. All criteria met. Server serves React SPA, static assets, favicon. Old /dashboard and /narrative preserved. API routes take priority. CLAUDE.md documented.

features.name: App layout shell + dark theme
features.description: Create the app layout: sidebar navigation (collapsible), top bar with project title + status indicator, and main content area. Apply dark theme (bg #0d1117, gold accents #e3b341) using Tailwind config. Mobile responsive with sidebar as drawer on small screens.
features.notes: Reviewed 2026-03-26. All criteria met. Three-tier responsive layout (mobile bottom nav, tablet drawer, desktop sidebar), dark theme, gold highlights, 44px+ touch targets, zero console errors.

features.name: Scene Library page
features.description: Build the home/landing page that lists all saved scenes (stories). Fetch from GET /api/stories. Display as a grid of cards showing: title, theme badge, difficulty, room count, agent count, creation date, save count. Include a 'New Scene' button and delete action per card. Empty state when no scenes exist.
features.notes: Reviewed 2026-03-27. All criteria met. Cards show title, theme badge, difficulty, creation date, save count. Responsive grid (1/2/3 cols). Delete with confirmation sheet. Empty/loading/error states. Room/agent count not available from API — save_count and max_tick shown instead (acceptable adaptation).

features.name: WebSocket connection + Zustand store
features.description: Create a Zustand store for game state (world state, agents, rooms, escape chain, ticks, connection status). Implement a WebSocket hook that connects to ws://localhost:8741/ws and dispatches incoming messages to the store. Handle reconnection on disconnect. Export typed selectors for all state slices.
features.notes: Reviewed 2026-03-26. All criteria met. Types match backend models exactly. Store handles all 6 WS message types. Reconnect with exponential backoff. 22 typed selectors exported. Build clean.

features.name: Game Monitor — Narrative Feed
features.description: Build the narrative feed panel for the game monitor page. Renders narrator-generated prose as styled cards (one per tick). Supports auto-scroll (follow latest) and manual scroll. Filter by event type (puzzle, movement, dialogue, discovery). Show tick number on each card.
features.notes: Re-reviewed 2026-03-26 after scroll containment fix. All criteria met. Auto-scroll, manual pause, Jump-to-latest, filters, tick badges all functional. Build clean.

features.name: Game Monitor — Agent Status Strip
features.description: Build a horizontal agent status strip showing each agent as a compact card: name, current room, inventory icons, emotional state indicator, current action. Updates in real-time from the Zustand store. Clicking an agent card expands to show more detail.
features.notes: Reviewed 2026-03-26. All criteria met. Cards show name, room, inventory count. Detail bottom-sheet (mobile) / dialog (desktop). Backdrop, Escape, X dismiss all work. useMemo workaround solid. Backend lacks emotional_state/current_action fields — reasonable adaptation.

features.name: Game Monitor — Escape Chain Progress Bar
features.description: Build a visual escape chain progress component. Show the 12-step chain as a horizontal segmented bar with solved (green), active (gold pulse), and pending (gray) states. Below the bar, show a collapsible checklist with step descriptions. Clicking a solved step shows which agent solved it and at what tick.
features.notes: Reviewed 2026-03-26. All criteria met. Segmented bar with green/gold-pulse/gray states. Collapsible checklist. Solved step detail (agent + tick). Touch targets 48-60px. ARIA progressbar. Collapsed 49px. Hides when no data. Build clean.

features.name: Game Monitor — Simulation Controls
features.description: Build play/pause/step control bar. Play button calls POST /api/resume, pause calls POST /api/pause, step calls POST /api/step. Show current tick count and simulation speed. Disable step button when not paused. Add a 'Quit to Library' button that pauses and navigates home.
features.notes: Re-reviewed 2026-03-27 after optimistic state fix. All criteria met. Play/Pause toggle, Step disable, tick counter, Quit-to-Library all verified.

features.name: Quick Play flow
features.description: Implement a 'Quick Play' button on the Scene Library that calls POST /api/stories/create with a random theme + default settings, then navigates to the Game Monitor page. Also wire up the 'Play' button on existing scene cards to call POST /api/stories/{id}/play and navigate to monitor.
features.notes: Reviewed 2026-03-27. All criteria met. Quick Play calls POST /api/stories/create with random theme, shows generation overlay, navigates to /monitor. Play button on cards calls POST /api/stories/{id}/play and navigates. Full E2E loop verified. Touch targets 44px+. Build clean.

features.name: Scene Creator — Story tab
features.description: Build the Scene Creator page with a tabbed layout (Story | Rooms | Puzzles | Agents | Validate). The Story tab has: theme selector (card-based, fetch from GET /api/themes), premise text area, difficulty slider (1-5), and a 'Generate World Bible' button that calls POST /api/generate-story and displays the result (setting, characters, inciting incident).

features.name: Scene Creator — Room graph editor
features.description: Build the Rooms tab with a visual graph editor (React Flow or similar). Users can: add rooms (click to create node), connect rooms with doors (drag edge), select a room to edit name/description/entities in a side panel. Show door status (locked/unlocked) on edges. Include an 'Auto-generate' button that uses the world bible to populate rooms.

features.name: Scene Creator — Puzzle builder tab
features.description: Build the Puzzles tab. List all puzzles in the scene with: name, type (dropdown: key_lock, combination_lock, password_door, pressure_plate, examine_reveal, sequential_levers), room assignment, required items/clues. Add/remove puzzles. Visual dependency chain showing which puzzles gate which others. AI-assist button: describe puzzle intent in natural language, Claude generates the mechanics.

features.name: Scene Creator — Agent designer tab
features.description: Build the Agents tab. Add/remove agents with: name, role, backstory textarea, personality traits (tag input from archetype library + free text), spawn room (dropdown from rooms), initial inventory, goal, and secret motivation. Show relationship matrix between agents (trust slider per pair). Preview how traits map to behavior.

features.name: Scene Creator — Validate tab + save scene
features.description: Build the Validate tab. Run solvability check (call a new POST /api/validate-scene endpoint or client-side BFS). Show pass/fail per puzzle with explanation. 'Save Scene' button persists to the story database. 'Play Scene' button saves + starts the game + navigates to monitor.

features.name: Puzzle Progress Dashboard panel
features.description: Add a collapsible panel to the Game Monitor showing all puzzles in a grid: puzzle name, type icon, room, status badge (unsolved/in-progress/solved), clues found vs required. Warmth indicator (cold/warm/hot) based on clue discovery ratio. Click a puzzle to see its clue list and which agents found which clues.

features.name: Interactive Map panel
features.description: Add an interactive room map to the Game Monitor. Render room graph with React Flow (read-only mode). Show agent positions as animated markers on their current room. Color doors by status (locked red, unlocked green). Click a room to see entity list and current occupants. Optional heat map toggle (room visit frequency).

features.name: Save/Load UI in Game Monitor
features.description: Add save/load controls to the Game Monitor. 'Save' button (with optional name input) calls POST /api/save. 'Load' dropdown lists saves for current story (GET /api/stories/{id}) with tick number and timestamp. Loading calls POST /api/load/{id} and refreshes the store. Show auto-save indicator.

features.name: Agent thought bubbles
features.description: Show agent reasoning in the UI. When an agent makes a decision, display a thought bubble near their status card with a summarized version of the LLMBrain reasoning (key observation + chosen action + why). Thought bubbles fade after a few seconds or on next tick. Toggle on/off per agent.

features.name: Agent memory inspector
features.description: Add a slide-out panel per agent showing: working memory (always-visible key facts), memory stream (scrollable list sorted by recency, with importance score badges), and reflections (periodic summaries). Search within agent memory. Highlight which memories were retrieved for the latest decision.

features.name: Agent perception overlay
features.description: When viewing an agent's detail panel, show what the agent currently perceives: a filtered view of the room showing only visible entities (respecting HIDDEN state), available exits, other agents present, and the agent's own inventory. Contrast with the 'god view' (everything in the room) to show what the agent is missing.

features.name: Nudge system
features.description: Add spectator nudge controls: 'Hint' (suggest an agent examine a specific entity), 'Focus' (redirect an agent to a specific puzzle), 'Talk' (prompt two agents to discuss a topic). Nudges are sent as a new WebSocket message type or API call. Show nudge history in a collapsible log. Limit nudge frequency (cooldown timer).

features.name: Agent conversation log
features.description: Dedicated panel showing all agent-to-agent conversations (talk actions). Display as a chat-style UI with agent avatars and speech bubbles. Filter by agent pair. Highlight conversations where clues were shared or puzzle information was exchanged.

features.name: Timeline scrubber + replay
features.description: Add a timeline bar to the Game Monitor showing all ticks. Event markers on the timeline for: puzzle solves (star), discoveries (magnifying glass), conversations (speech bubble). Click a marker to jump to that tick's narrative card. Scrub backward through saved state (requires auto-saves at interval).

features.name: Save branching
features.description: When loading a mid-game save, create a named branch. Show a branch tree visualization in the save/load UI. Each branch tracks its own escape chain progress independently. Allow switching between branches.

features.name: Scene export/import
features.description: Export a scene definition as a JSON file (download). Import a scene from a JSON file (upload + validate + create story). Include all scene data: rooms, entities, puzzles, agents, escape chain, world bible. Validate imported JSON schema before accepting.

features.name: Scene duplicate + version history
features.description: Add a 'Duplicate' button on scene cards in the library. Track scene edits as versions (store diffs or snapshots). Show version history in the scene creator. Revert to a previous version.
